subject: [PATCH v2 2/3] fuse: use release/acquire for fch->initialized

2026-07-15.patch_summary: This patch addresses a potential issue in the FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) kernel module where lockless readers may access the 'initialized' flag of a fuse channel without proper synchronization, leading to undefined behavior. To fix this, the patch introduces release/acquire semantics for writing and reading the 'initialized' flag using smp_store_release() and smp_load_acquire(). This ensures that writers and readers are properly synchronized, preventing potential issues. The changes are made in several files: cuse.c, dev.c, and dev_uring.c. Although this issue is not considered critical and may only be triggered by buggy or malicious servers, the patch aims to improve the overall robustness of the FUSE module.
